title: Dog Capitals
text:
About
Dog Capitals is an independent quantitative research initiative focused on systematic volatility-targeting strategies. We build rules-based investment programs grounded in a single core thesis: the S&P 500 is the best asset to trade, and volatility is the best tool to manage it.
All strategies share the same framework — downside volatility targeting, dynamic leverage, and signal-lagged execution. Each program expresses that framework across a different market, capital size, and investor type.
Programs are developed entirely in-house. Backtest evidence and live performance are reported without adjustment.

title: Dog Capitals
text:
Programs

ES Core Relative Allocation
U.S. large-cap equity futures (ES) as a permanent core with dynamic allocation to eight alternatives across equities, fixed income, real assets, and digital assets. Downside volatility targeting in the 10–20% range. Daily execution via Interactive Brokers.
Backtest (2003–2026): CAGR 9.54% · Sharpe 0.592 · Max DD -36.48%
Strategy document →



ETF Vol Targeting
Monthly rebalancing across four CEDEAR instruments (SPY, QQQ, GLD, IBIT) using the same vol targeting framework. S&P 500 as permanent core with a hard floor. No leverage. Designed for Argentine investors seeking USD-denominated returns with capital gains tax efficiency.
Backtest (2005–2026): CAGR 21.46% · Sharpe 1.41 · Max DD -15.47%
Strategy document →

The conceptual foundation underlying each program is documented in the strategy papers linked above. The shared framework covers volatility estimation, signal construction, portfolio construction, and execution.
